# Brief: S5-D1 — context assembly + guarded provider in ConfigResolution

## Task

In `packages/1-framework/3-tooling/language-server/src/config-resolution.ts` (with
minimal `ProjectState` threading in `server.ts`): when the loaded config has PSL
inputs and `config.contract?.source` passes `hasPslInterpreter` (imported from
`@prisma-next/psl-parser/interpret`), extend `ConfigResolution` with an optional
grouped member (e.g. `interpretation?: { source: PslInterpretCapable; context:
ContractSourceContext }`) — grouped, so downstream code has one presence check.
Assemble the context once per config (re)load by property picks off the same
`createControlStack(config)` call the partial-inputs derivation already makes
(restructure `resolveControlStackInputs` so the stack is created once, not twice):
`composedExtensionPacks: stack.extensionPacks.map(p => p.id)`,
`composedExtensionContracts: stack.extensionContracts`, `scalarTypeDescriptors`,
`authoringContributions`, `codecLookup`, `controlMutationDefaults`, `capabilities`,
and `resolvedInputs` from the contract source's `inputs` resolved the way the LSP
resolves schema inputs today (absolute paths per `resolveSchemaInputs` — reuse, don't
re-derive). No consumer uses the new member yet (dead until S5-D2). Configs without
the capability (typescript/opaque source, no contract, no PSL inputs) get
`interpretation: undefined` and identical behavior to today.

Thread the member through `ProjectState` in `server.ts` (carry, don't consume).

Tests first (`test/config-resolution.test.ts` has the existing patterns): capability
present → member populated, `context.composedExtensionContracts` is the stack's map
(reference or content pin), pack ids match `extensionPacks` order; capability absent
(typescript source / no contract) → member undefined; stack created once (spy or
structural evidence, implementer's judgment).

## Scope

**In:** `language-server/src/{config-resolution,server}.ts` + language-server tests.
**Out:** diagnostic-mapping, project-artifacts, document-diagnostics, pipeline —
all S5-D2; no behavior change to any existing response; `projects/**`; GitHub threads.

## Completed when

- [ ] `ConfigResolution` (and `ProjectState`) carry the optional grouped member;
      populated/absent per the guard; assembled by property picks (zero casts).
- [ ] Tests per above; all existing language-server tests untouched and green.
- [ ] Gates: `pnpm --filter @prisma-next/language-server test` + typecheck + lint,
      `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint:deps`.

## Standing instruction

Stay focused; control scope. Invoke nothing on the provider (that is D2's job — and
when D2 comes: always call `interpret` as a method, never detach it).

## Operational metadata

- **Time-box:** 60 min. Halt: any context property resists a cast-free pick (a
  slice-02/03 gap — surface); `hasPslInterpreter` fails to narrow the loaded config's
  source (would mean the config loader erases the provider type — surface with the
  evidence, do not cast around it).
